rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL JIMENEZ VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS, et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 3, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Rosendahl Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's insufficient questioning of plaintiff during deposition precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. However even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and the declaration of Steven Compton, Ph.D. would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (asbestos welding gloves and blankets) attributable to defendant. Defendant does not dispute that the welding gloves and blankets used by its employees contained asbestos, for purposes of this motion. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating the causation element of plaintiff's claim because it did not present evidence to demonstrate that any asbestos exposure attributable to defendant was not, in reasonable medical probability, a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease(s). (Rutherford v. Owens-Illinois, Inc. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 953.) Plaintiff's objections to the declaration of William Ringo, Ph.D., CIH, CSP are sustained. However, even if Dr. Ringo's declaration was admissible in its entirety it would be insufficient to make the requisite showing on this issue. The Court will not consider defendant's August 28, 2015 supplemental reply brief or the August 31, 2015 second supplemental Strunk declaration because they are untimely, violate plaintiff's due process rights and the purported deposition transcript is a rough draft. Plaintiff's objections to these documents are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL JIMENEZ VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS, et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 3, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant McGuire & Hester Corporation's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether the cement pipe at issue contained asbestos. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Charles Ay are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SWEIDER SR. et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 3, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Trena Vick is denied as untimely. Brayton failed to provide plaintiff with proper notice as required by C.C.P. section 1005(b), which requires all moving and supporting papers to be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing, which notice period is increased by 10 calendar days if the notice is served by mail to a place outside California. Here, Brayton provided plaintiff notice by mail to an address in Oregon on August 7, 2015, which did not provide proper notice. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)
